response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has 15+ years of strong IC technical experience, relevant developer tooling work (test runners, assertion libraries, CI/CD pipelines, MCP infrastructure at Google), and informal leadership through workshops and Staff-level roles. However, this is explicitly a full-time people-leadership role requiring demonstrated people management, and no evidence of managing direct reports, conducting performance reviews, or running structured 1:1s appears anywhere in the resume. Every position listed is individual-contributor, even at Staff/Senior level, making the gap to a Level II Engineering Manager role a critical disqualifier.
strengths: 15+ years of deep polyglot engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure — strong technical foundation for credibility with ICs
Directly relevant developer tooling output: open-source test runner (Trial), assertion library (Fluent-Asserts), CI/CD setup at Altom, and MCP code generators at Google
Consistent knowledge-sharing track record through workshops, conference talks, and open-source projects — signals coaching and communication instincts
concerns: Zero demonstrable people management experience — no direct reports, no performance conversations, no hiring or firing decisions mentioned across 16+ years
All roles including the current Google Staff Engineer position are individual-contributor; no indication of even informal team-lead or tech-lead-manager hybrid experience
Mobile platform knowledge is limited and dated — the only mobile work is Apple TV/iOS at WatchUp in 2015–2016, far short of the depth expected for a productivity role serving a major mobile ecosystem

key_factors.factor: Shallow and outdated mobile platform exposure
